en: I am a rose of Sharon, a lily of the valleys.
zh: 我是沙崙平原的水仙花， 是谷中的百合花。

en: As a lily among brambles, so is my love among the young women.
zh: 我的佳偶在女子中， 正像荊棘裡的百合花。

en: As an apple tree among the trees of the forest, so is my beloved among the young men. With great delight I sat in his shadow, and his fruit was sweet to my taste,
zh: 我的良人在男子中， 好像樹林裡的一棵蘋果樹； 我歡歡喜喜地坐在它的蔭下， 它的果子香甜合我口味。

en: He brought me to the banqueting house, and his banner over me was love.
zh: 他帶我進入酒室， 含情脈脈地望著我。

en: Sustain me with raisins; refresh me with apples, for I am sick with love.
zh: 求你們用葡萄餅增補我力， 用蘋果恢復我的精神， 因為我因愛成病。

en: His left hand is under my head, and his right hand embraces me!
zh: 他的左手托住我的頭， 他的右手擁抱著我。

en: I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem, by the gazelles or the does of the field, that you not stir up or awaken love until it pleases.
zh: 耶路撒冷的眾女子啊！ 我指著田野的羚羊、母鹿懇求你們， 不要激動，不要挑動愛情， 等它自發吧。

en: The voice of my beloved! Behold, he comes, leaping over the mountains, bounding over the hills.
zh: 聽啊！這是我良人的聲音， 他躥山越嶺而來。

en: My beloved is like a gazelle or a young stag. Behold, there he stands behind our wall, gazing through the windows, looking through the lattice.
zh: 我的良人像羚羊，像小鹿。 看哪！他站在我們的牆後； 從窗口觀看， 從窗格往裡面探視。

en: My beloved speaks and says to me: "Arise, my love, my beautiful one, and come away,
zh: 我的良人對我說： “我的佳偶啊！你起來吧。 我的佳麗啊！你來吧。

en: for behold, the winter is past; the rain is over and gone.
zh: 因為冬天已過， 雨水止息，已經過去了。

en: The flowers appear on the earth, the time of singing has come, and the voice of the turtledove is heard in our land.
zh: 地上百花齊放， 百鳥齊鳴的時候已經來到； 斑鳩的聲音在我們境內也聽見了。

en: The fig tree ripens its figs, and the vines are in blossom; they give forth fragrance. Arise, my love, my beautiful one, and come away.
zh: 無花果樹的果子漸漸成熟， 葡萄樹開花發香， 我的佳偶啊！你起來吧。 我的佳麗啊！你來吧。

en: O my dove, in the clefts of the rock, in the crannies of the cliff, let me see your face, let me hear your voice, for your voice is sweet, and your face is lovely.
zh: 我的鴿子啊！在巖石的穴中， 在懸崖石的隱密處， 讓我看看你的容貌； 讓我聽聽你的聲音； 因為你的聲音甜美， 你的容貌秀麗。

en: Catch the foxes for us, the little foxes that spoil the vineyards, for our vineyards are in blossom."
zh: 要為我們捉拿狐狸， 就是毀壞葡萄園的小狐狸， 因為我們的葡萄園正在開花。”

en: My beloved is mine, and I am his; he grazes among the lilies.
zh: 我的良人屬我，我也屬他， 他在百合花中細賞。

en: Until the day breathes and the shadows flee, turn, my beloved, be like a gazelle or a young stag on cleft mountains.
zh: 我的良人啊！ 等到天起涼風，日影消逝的時候， 願你歸來， 好像崎嶇山上的羚羊或是小鹿。
